mods.name: IFZ Mod API
mods.summary: Shared library other IFZMods depend on. Controller cache, time/night helpers, VFX + reflection utilities. Install if a mod lists it as required.

sections.description:
Shared helper library for Infection Free Zone BepInEx mods. Loads first (filename 000_IFZModAPI.dll). Provides a cached game-controller registry (Buildings / Groups / Squads / Stockrooms / Work / ColorSwitcher / Weather / Light), time + night-blend helpers, VFX helpers (real smoke prefab clone, pooled point lights), cached reflection, and a PostProcessing FloatParameter shim.

You only need this if another mod lists it as a requirement. It does nothing on its own.
sections.installation: Drop 000_IFZModAPI.dll into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Nothing to configure. Required by ArmyBackup, Cinematic FX, and Smart Worker Redistribution (and possibly others over time).
sections.mainFeatures:
- Loads before all other IFZMods (alphabetical + BepInDependency)
- Shared controller-instance cache
- Time / sunrise / sunset / NightBlend helpers
- VFX: real Smoke prefab clone, pooled point-light flashes
- Cached reflection + FloatParameter shim
sections.requirements: BepInEx 5.4.23.2. Infection Free Zone Unity 6000.0.26 / beta 0.26.5.29+. Pure-managed netstandard2.1 — Windows / macOS (Crossover) / Steam Deck (Proton).
sections.shoutOuts: Built by JayMade. Source: https://github.com/JaySNL/IFZMods.

mods.name: Army Backup AI Fix
mods.summary: Request Backup Army tanks actually engage hostiles near your base instead of idling.
mods.description:
Fixes the broken "Request Backup" AI. Vanilla Army tanks spawn and sit. With this mod they sweep player buildings on a configurable tick, find the nearest Infected or Bandit threat inside BaseDefenceRadius, and issue an AttackGroupOrder via the game's own OrdersHandlers. Includes hysteresis (ReassignDistance) so they don't dither between targets, separate toggles for Infected vs Bandits, configurable engagement range, and verbose logging.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled toggle in `[General]`. Tickrate, radii, target faction filter, hysteresis distance all live-tunable.

sections.description: Fixes the broken "Request Backup" AI. Vanilla Army backup tanks spawn and sit. With this mod, every 4 seconds (configurable) the mod walks all Army groups, finds the nearest Infected or Bandit threat inside the player base radius, and issues an AttackGroupOrder via the game's own OrdersHandlers.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Auto-engage hostiles near player base
- Hysteresis (ReassignDistance) prevents target dithering
- Separate toggles for Infected vs Bandits
- Configurable engagement range, tick rate, base defence radius
- F1 ConfigurationManager support, master Enabled toggle

mods.name: Cinematic FX
mods.summary: Buffed blood and tracers, demolish dust, impact craters, night thunderstorms.
mods.description:
Visual polish pass. Five independent subsystems, each toggleable:

- **Burning Structures** — buildings below configurable HP threshold emit cloned in-game smoke from their roof.
- **Buffed Blood** — scales blood splash particle size and emission rate.
- **Buffed Tracers** — widens bullet tracer LineRenderer.
- **Demolish Dust** — dust burst on deconstruct completion.
- **Impact Craters** — extra smoke puff on explosion sites.
- **Night Storms** — thunder rolls during night after configurable game day.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`, per-feature toggles under each subsystem section.

sections.description: Visual polish pass: burning structures, buffed blood and tracers, demolish dust, impact craters, night thunderstorms. Five independent subsystems, each individually toggleable.
sections.installation:

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Burning Structures: smoke from damaged buildings below HP threshold
- Buffed Blood: scales splash particle size and emission
- Buffed Tracers: wider tracer LineRenderer
- Demolish Dust: dust burst on deconstruct completion
- Impact Craters: smoke puff on explosion sites
- Night Storms: thunder rolls after configurable game day

mods.name: Construction ETA
mods.summary: Shows time-remaining on build, repair, and deconstruct panels.
mods.description:
Adds an estimated time-to-completion label to every active construction, repair, and deconstruction work panel. Reads from the same work-progress fields the game uses internally; no balance change. Useful for planning when crews will free up.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description: Adds an estimated time-to-completion label to every active construction, repair, and deconstruction work panel. Reads the same work-progress fields the game uses internally. Zero balance change, pure UI overlay.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- ETA on Build / Repair / Deconstruct panels
- Pulls live from the game's own progress data
- Useful for planning when crews free up

mods.name: Darker Nights
mods.summary: Actually-dark nights, full-moon brightness bonus, smooth dawn/dusk, day tone-shaping.
mods.description:
Owns the day/night lighting curve. Patches the game's ColorSwitcher source fields (`saturation_Day_PP`, `contrast_Day_PP`, `blue_Day_PP`) so the per-tick lerp uses our values as the basis — outputs don't get clobbered by the game.

- **Night** — configurable sun-intensity factor (0 = pitch black, 1 = vanilla), absolute ambient color + intensity, AutoExposure clamp. Full-moon nights apply a brightness multiplier on top.
- **Day** — saturation / contrast / warmth / post-exposure boost so daylight doesn't feel flat. Tunable per slider.
- **Smooth transitions** — configurable TransitionHours fade window around sunrise / sunset. Pair with GunfireLights' matching value for synchronized tower-light fade-in.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description:
Owns the day/night lighting curve. Patches the game's ColorSwitcher source fields (saturation_Day_PP, contrast_Day_PP, blue_Day_PP) so the per-tick lerp uses our values as the basis — outputs don't get clobbered by the game.

Nights are actually dark. Days don't feel flat. Transitions are smooth.

- Full-moon nights apply a brightness multiplier
- Day tone-shaping: saturation / contrast / warmth / post-exposure
- Smooth dawn/dusk via configurable TransitionHours
- Pairs with GunfireLights for synchronised tower-light fade-in

mods.name: Cancel Paused Deconstruction
mods.summary: Cancel paused deconstruction tasks — vanilla blocks the delete button on partial demolitions.
mods.description:
Vanilla hides the delete button on deconstructions once they're paused (partial demolition). This mod keeps the button visible and forces a clean delete on click — state machine reverts to pre-deconstruct, work entry is cleared, structure remains.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description: Vanilla hides the delete button on deconstructions once they're paused (partial demolition), so you can't cancel them. This mod keeps the button visible and forces a clean delete on click — state machine reverts to pre-deconstruct, work entry is cleared, structure remains intact.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Delete button stays visible on paused deconstructions
- Forces clean delete (state machine reverts, work entry cleared)
- No leftover state on the structure

mods.name: Explosives Planting Unlock
mods.summary: Force-unlocks explosives_planting so the open-beta demolition feature actually works.
mods.description:
The beta exposes explosives planting in the UI but the content flag is locked, so the planting action no-ops. This mod calls `UnlockContentById("explosives_planting")` after the game's ContentStateController validates missing content. Single Harmony postfix, zero side effects.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description: The IFZ open beta exposes explosives planting in the UI but the content flag is locked, so the planting action no-ops. This mod unlocks the explosives_planting content flag after the game's ContentStateController validates missing content.
sections.installation:

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Single Harmony postfix, zero side effects
- Unlocks explosives planting in current and new saves
- No balance change otherwise

mods.name: Gunfire Lights
mods.summary: Real-time point lights for muzzle flashes, explosions, headlights, tower searchlights, antenna beacons.
mods.description:
Adds dynamic lighting to combat and infrastructure:

- **Muzzle flashes** — pooled point light per gun, weapon-specific color tint (heavy / shotgun warmer).
- **Explosions** — orange flash with configurable range and intensity.
- **Vehicle headlights** — forward-facing spot light on moving vehicles at night.
- **Tower searchlights** — slow-sweeping spot light on defensive towers, holds on last-attacked target.
- **Antenna aviation beacons** — real-world FAA-style red/green blinking lights on antenna towers, 24/7 or night-only.

All lights fade in/out across a configurable TransitionHours window around sunrise / sunset. Pair with DarkerNights' matching value for synchronized lighting.

Light pool of 32 reused across all flashes. No shadow casting (cheap).

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description: Real-time dynamic lighting for combat and infrastructure. Muzzle flashes on every gun, explosion flashes, vehicle headlights at night, tower searchlights that sweep and lock on, red/green aviation beacons on antenna towers.
sections.installation:

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.

- Explosions: orange flash with configurable range
- Vehicle headlights: forward spot light on moving vehicles at night
- Tower searchlights: slow-sweeping spot, holds on last-attacked target
- Antenna beacons: real-world FAA-style red/green blinking, 24/7 or night-only
- Smooth fade-in/out around sunrise / sunset via TransitionHours
- 32-light pool, no shadow casting (cheap)

mods.name: House Power
mods.summary: Powered homes get a +mood bonus, chimneys emit smoke, entrance lights at night.
mods.description:
Residential buildings auto-install a power generator that consumes wood on a configurable tick. While powered:

- **+mood bonus** — separate row in the mood panel, scales with the fraction of residents in powered homes. Live UI update.
- **Chimney smoke** — clones the in-game worksite smoke at chimney prop, falls back to roof centre if no chimney prop. High-rises without chimneys skipped.
- **Entrance / window lights** — small warm point lights at door and window props, brighter at night, dimmer at day. Throttled to 2 Hz to keep cost down across hundreds of buildings.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Wood-per-tick, tick interval, mood bonus magnitude, light range and intensity all tunable.

sections.description: Powered homes get a separate +mood bonus, chimneys emit smoke, entrance lights glow at night. Residential buildings auto-install a generator that burns wood on a configurable tick. While powered, residents are happier and the house actually looks lived-in.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- +mood bonus shown in the mood panel, scales with fraction of residents in powered homes
- Chimney smoke on powered houses (or roof centre fallback, skips high-rises without chimneys)
- Entrance / window lights on at night, dimmer during day
- Throttled to 2 Hz across hundreds of buildings for low perf cost
- Tunable wood-per-tick and tick interval

mods.name: House Rebalance
mods.summary: Citizens auto-migrate to higher-priority housing when capacity exists.
mods.description:
Vanilla citizens stay assigned to whatever house they got first. With this mod, every TickSeconds (default 30) each citizen re-evaluates housing via the game's own `FindBestHouse` ranking — they walk to a better house if one has slots. No more "want better housing" mood penalty while empty mansions sit unused.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Tick interval tunable.

sections.description:
Vanilla citizens stay assigned to whatever house they got first. With this mod, every TickSeconds (default 30) each citizen re-evaluates housing using the game's own FindBestHouse ranking and migrates to a better house if slots exist.

Result: no more "want better housing" mood penalty while empty mansions sit unused.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Periodic citizen re-housing sweep
- Uses the game's native FindBestHouse priority
- Eliminates "want better housing" complaints with empty premium housing
- Tick interval tunable in F1

mods.name: IFZ Quality of Life Bundle
mods.summary: Bundle of small QoL tweaks: TowerHMG, VehicleAmmoFix, HeightAdvantage, BuildingIconToggle, ShellLairs, ClayPitRegen. Each toggleable in F1.
mods.fileNotes: New in 1.3.1: select a bunker/tower with a mortar weapon, right-click a revealed lair, and it shells the lair on its own cooldown until destroyed (toggle [ShellLairs] Bunkers). Also fixes BuildingIconToggle so it hides structure icons again.
mods.description:
Several small quality-of-life tweaks bundled in one DLL. Each feature is individually toggleable in F1 ConfigurationManager.

Main features:
- TowerHMG: defensive towers accept Heavy MGs (eq_hcal) once carworkshop_hcal is researched.
- VehicleAmmoFix: vehicles auto-refill ammo from their own trunk cargo when the stockroom path fails.
- HeightAdvantage: squads in tall buildings get a fight-range bonus scaled by building height.
- BuildingIconToggle: hotkey (default I) to hide/show all building icons; state persists.
- ShellLairs: vanilla won't let you attack-order infected lairs. With mortar squad(s) selected, right-click a revealed infected lair to shell it; plain infantry still enter to clear as normal. Selected bunkers/towers that have a mortar weapon also shell the lair on the same right-click.
- ClayPitRegen: depleted clay pits regenerate production to ~50% after a configurable number of days, and a depleted pit can be demolished to reclaim the land.

mods.name: Locale Fix (ConfigurationManager floats)
mods.summary: Forces InvariantCulture so ConfigurationManager can edit decimal floats on comma-locale systems.
mods.description:
Required if you're on a comma-decimal Windows locale (nl-NL, de-DE, fr-FR, etc.) and you've seen `FormatException: Input string was not in a correct format` when editing float fields in the F1 ConfigurationManager UI.

Forces `CultureInfo.InvariantCulture` on the main thread and `DefaultThreadCurrentCulture`. A Sentinel MonoBehaviour re-pins every frame in case Unity / game subsystems reset it on scene loads.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Note: the Awake-time pin applies once at process start regardless of toggle. Off-during-session stops the per-frame Sentinel re-pin but doesn't undo the initial pin.

sections.description:
Required on comma-decimal Windows locales (nl-NL, de-DE, fr-FR, etc.) where editing float fields in BepInEx ConfigurationManager throws FormatException ("Input string was not in a correct format").

Forces CultureInfo.InvariantCulture on the main thread + DefaultThreadCurrentCulture. A Sentinel MonoBehaviour re-pins every frame in case Unity / game subsystems reset it on scene loads.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.

- One-time culture pin on Awake
- Per-frame Sentinel re-pin (cheap branch + pointer compare)
- Fixes float-parse FormatException in ConfigurationManager UI
- Caveat: the Awake-time pin applies once at process start regardless of toggle

mods.name: Performance Pack
mods.summary: Late-game FPS fixes. Headline: hideout patrol spawn-point LOD ~doubles night FPS on old saves — same horde, no gameplay change.
mods.fileNotes: 1.4.1: Hideout patrol spawn-point LOD - ~2x late-game night FPS (deep save ~13 -> ~28), full swarm intact. Off-screen patrol movement staggered + inert debug colliders disabled. Fixes a 1.4.0 bug that suppressed off-screen attacks.
mods.description:
A bundle of safe, measured late-game performance patches. Headline fix (1.4.0):

- **Hideout patrol spawn-point LOD** — old/large maps accumulate tens of thousands of dormant patrol spawn points (the game's lightweight 'virtual' form of a patrol). The base game updates EVERY one every frame (path-move + terrain raycast + fog) and leaves a debug-only physics collider live on each — at ~19k points that was the single biggest night-time cost (measured ~50ms/frame of it). PerfPack distance-LODs them: off-screen points update less often with time-compensated movement (roaming/despawn stay accurate), near-camera points are untouched, and the inert debug colliders are disabled. **Horde size is unchanged** — same number of patrols, they just stop burning the CPU while off-screen. Roughly **doubled night FPS** on a deep save (12-13 -> 26-28) with no perceptible gameplay change.

Also included:

- **A\* graph-update throttle** — spreads navmesh rebuilds (build/demolish/wall-damage) across frames instead of one fat main-thread stall.
- **Enemy 'nearest building' AI cache** — the raid/siege AI re-scans a group's entire in-range building set every behaviour tick; cached per group for a short TTL.
- **Billboard throttle** — refreshes each `Billboard.Update` every Nth frame, staggered.
- **Blood-decay throttle** — clamps `SetBloodPuddleValue` writes to once every IntervalSec per body.
- **PerfScheduler** — frame-budget drain queue + stagger foundation.
- **Opt-in diagnostics** (off by default) — frame-spike profiler + per-method CPU sampler for diagnosing hitches.

All patches are config-gated and reversible. Safe defaults — no rendering or logic regressions.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`, per-feature toggles under each section.

sections.description: Safe, measured late-game performance patches. Headline (1.4.0): hideout patrol spawn-point LOD roughly doubles night FPS on old saves with no gameplay change (same horde). Plus A* graph-update throttle, enemy nearest-building AI cache, billboard/blood throttles, and opt-in diagnostics.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Hideout patrol spawn-point LOD (1.4.0): off-screen dormant patrols updated less often with time-compensated movement; near-camera untouched; inert debug colliders disabled. Same horde size, ~2x night FPS on deep saves.
- A* graph-update throttle: spreads navmesh rebuilds across frames
- Enemy nearest-building AI cache: short-TTL cache of the raid/siege building scan
- Billboard throttle: refreshes each billboard every Nth frame, staggered
- Blood decay throttle: clamps SetBloodPuddleValue writes per body
- Opt-in diagnostics (off by default): frame-spike profiler + per-method CPU sampler

BepInEx 5.4.23.2. Infection Free Zone Unity 6000.0.26 / beta 0.26.5.29+. Pure-managed netstandard2.1 — runs on Windows / macOS (Crossover) / Steam Deck (Proton).

mods.name: Save File Version Unlock
mods.summary: Bypasses the Unsupported save file version check. Load older saves on newer game builds.
mods.description:
Patches `VersionValidator.ValidatePlayedVersions` and `ValidateMinPlayableVersion` to always return true. Lets you load saves from older game builds on a newer build without the "Unsupported save file" gate.

**Caveat:** schema-breaking saves may still fail mid-load. Back up your save before trying. This mod removes the gate, not the underlying schema differences.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description:

mods.name: Smart Worker Redistribution
mods.summary: Stalled construction sites auto-pull workers from idle jobs.
mods.description:
Every TickSeconds (default 20), scans all active works. Counts vacancies on unsuspended works that can execute. Frees an equal number of workers from suspended works (e.g. waiting on resources) — WorkController auto-reassigns them to live jobs.

Result: no more workers sitting idle at a stalled construction while another site needs them.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Tick interval tunable.

sections.description: When construction stalls because resources are missing but workers are still parked there, this mod frees them. Every TickSeconds (default 20) it counts vacancies on unsuspended works that can execute, then frees an equal number of workers from suspended works — WorkController auto-reassigns them to live jobs.
sections.installation:

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.

- Frees idle workers from stalled construction

mods.name: Split Unlock - castles and cathedrals
mods.summary: Bypass "too narrow / too complex" on splits, plus override the custom-building size limits.
mods.description:
Lets you cut up castles, cathedrals, and other irregular generated shapes that the vanilla split validator rejects.

The game's `BuildingSplitHandler.CheckWrongDimensions` enforces three thresholds via `SplitConfig`:
- polygon surface ≤ MinSplitSurface × 3 → SmallSurface
- offset-polygon shrink by WrongDimensionDelta produces ≠1 sub-poly → WrongDimensions
- any source node further than MaxDistanceFromOffsetPolygon from the shrunk poly → WrongDimensions

This mod flips a denied result to None when you opt in. Two independent toggles: `AllowWrongDimensions` (the narrow/complex check) and `AllowSmallSurface` (the size check), both default on.

**Caveat:** splits along genuinely degenerate geometry may produce ugly or partially-walkable split parts. The mod doesn't make those splits better — it lets them happen.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description:
Bypasses "Building parts contain too narrow or too complex elements" on building splits. Lets you cut up castles, cathedrals, and irregular generated shapes that the vanilla split validator rejects.

Caveat: splits along genuinely degenerate geometry may produce ugly or partially-walkable parts. The mod doesn't make those splits better — it lets them happen.

v1.1.0 also exposes the draw-your-own custom-building size limits (MaxBuildingSurface / MinBuildingSurface; vanilla 1000 / 5 m²). Defaults match vanilla, so it's opt-in — raise the max to build bigger. Only the size gate is lifted; wall-length / angle / slope / intersection checks still apply.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Two split toggles: AllowWrongDimensions (narrow/complex) and AllowSmallSurface (size), both default on
- NEW: custom-building size override — MaxBuildingSurface (vanilla 1000 m²) and MinBuildingSurface (vanilla 5 m²); raise the max to draw bigger custom buildings
- All tunable in F1 ConfigurationManager

mods.name: Squad Auto Behavior
mods.summary: Auto-medbay when wounded, auto-resupply ammo, auto-return to the nearest ammo source when dry.
mods.fileNotes: New in 1.1.0: when a squad runs dry it now heads to the NEAREST reachable building that holds ammo (a warehouse next door beats trekking back to HQ), and falls back to HQ only if no closer ammo source is reachable. Less cross-map babysitting.
mods.description:
Every TickSeconds (default 8) the mod sweeps player squads:

- **Auto-return when wounded** — wounded squad outside HQ moves to nearest building with a soldiers-treatment module and available beds.
- **Auto-resupply** — squad inside a HQ-zone enterable auto-grabs ammo from the stockroom when below LowAmmoFraction.
- **Auto-return when dry** — squad outside HQ with avg ammo below ReturnAmmoFraction is sent to the NEAREST reachable building holding ammo (a closer warehouse beats the HQ); falls back to HQ if none is reachable.

Daylight-only by default (configurable). Per-squad cooldowns prevent order spam.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Each behaviour individually toggleable.

sections.description: Reduces babysitting. Every TickSeconds (default 8) the mod sweeps player squads and issues smart orders: wounded squads go to medbay, indoor squads grab ammo from stockrooms, dry squads head to the nearest building that holds ammo (HQ as a fallback).
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Auto-medbay when wounded near HQ
- Auto-resupply ammo from stockroom indoors
- Auto-return to the NEAREST building with ammo when dry (HQ as fallback)
- Daylight-only by default (configurable)
- Per-squad cooldowns prevent order spam

mods.name: Squad Merge (right-click)
mods.summary: Right-click your own squad with another selected → merge into target squad.
mods.description:
Right-click an own-faction squad while another is selected → members transfer into the target (vanilla would have issued a follow order). Soft-cap merged squad size to configurable max (default 8 — above vanilla 4).

Long-distance: source squad follows the target first, auto-merges when within MergeRange (default 10m).

**Notes:**
- Vehicle merges are handled by VehicleSquadSize, not this mod.
- RequireShift exists but is off by default — vanilla hijacks Shift to switch the cursor to area-scavenge before the click registers, so it rarely works in practice.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. SoftCap, MergeRange tunable.

sections.description:

mods.name: Better Vehicle Squad Size
mods.summary: Vehicle capacity scales with cargo slots: 4 + floor(cargoSlots/4). Pickup 5, Van 6, Truck 8, Bus 9.
mods.description:
Vehicles carry their actual seat count instead of vanilla's flat 4. Formula: `4 + floor(cargoSlots / CARGO_SLOTS_PER_PERSON)` where CARGO_SLOTS_PER_PERSON = 4. Result: Pickup 5, Van 6, Truck 8, Bus 9.

- **Merge into vehicle** — right-click an owned vehicle with another squad selected → squad members transfer in (vanilla would just follow). Long-distance: follow first, merge inside 10m.
- **Split on exit** — when a vehicle squad with merged passengers disembarks, the extras become their own fully-functional squad, auto-named with a unique squad number, movable like any other squad.
- **UI patch** — squad panel shows `members / vehicleCap` while in vehicle.
- **Save / reload tested** — merged state persists without crash.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

sections.description:

mods.name: Surrounded - siege mode
mods.summary: Vanilla swarms attack far more often and bigger; scavenge loot multiplied so you push out between waves.

sections.description:
"Surrounded" siege mode. Day is the calm window; night is the assault.

It does NOT hand-spawn units (that produces engine-invalid groups that crash the game). Instead it steers the game's OWN swarm system — the swarms are 100% vanilla-built (correct pathing/targeting, no perf landmines), there are just a lot more of them, a lot more often.

Includes a day-1→30 ease-in ramp so a fresh base isn't run over instantly, and a read-only F1 Status panel that shows live exactly what it's changing for spawn frequency, horde size, and lair formation.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Siege: vanilla swarms attack much more frequently and bigger (SwarmFrequencyBoost, HordeSizeBoost)
- Difficulty ease-in ramp (RampDays, default 30) so day 1 isn't an instant loss
- Loot pull: scavenge + expedition yield multiplied (Loot.Multiplier) so you forage between assaults
- Read-only F1 Status panel: live effective spawn-frequency / horde-size / lair-formation numbers
- Optional opt-in lair-formation lever
- Everything live-tunable in F1; master toggle if it gets too brutal

mods.name: Hives - infected hive seeding (experimental)
mods.summary: Seeds extra infected hideouts across the map on a new game; they grow into lairs. Pairs with Surrounded.
mods.fileNotes: Fixed in 0.1.5: clearing a hive/lair then entering or standing by it no longer crashes. Lairs on ordinary buildings hit two game-side null crashes (scavenging it; squads reacting) that spammed crash logs - both now guarded. Includes 0.1.4 fix.

sections.description:
EXPERIMENTAL (v0.1.0). On a new game, seeds N buildings scattered across the map (away from your HQ) as infected hideouts using the game's OWN hideout spawn (engine-valid, not a hand-spawn). They mature into lairs over time, breeding local pressure — more scattered objectives to push out toward.

Seeds once per save (per-save flag, never re-stacks on reload). Loading a pre-Hives save seeds it fresh with your current settings. Pairs naturally with Surrounded (waves) — Hives gives you the map full of nests between them.
sections.installation:

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.
sections.mainFeatures:
- Seeds infected hives into buildings across the map on a new game
- Kept away from your start (MinDistanceFromHQ) so there's room to find them
- HiveCount (default 50), PlacePerTick tunable in F1
- Once per save — never re-stacks on reload
- Uses the engine's own hideout spawn (no crash-prone hand-spawns)

mods.summary: Raider camps grow when ignored, weaken when fought, raid your base, and drop loot when wiped.

sections.description:
Makes bandit/raider camps dynamic. Camps grow in strength when you ignore them, weaken when you fight them off, recoup over time, occasionally raid your base, and drop loot when wiped. Also pays out on cleared swarm nests.

Raiders spawn fully armed (they take the camp's own equipped weapon, with a guaranteed-armed floor). Fully tunable.

mods.name: Mass Deconstruct - drag-box
mods.summary: Press K, drag a box over your base, confirm — every qualifying building inside is queued for deconstruction at once.

sections.description:
Vanilla only lets you deconstruct one building at a time. This adds drag-box mass deconstruction: press K (configurable) to arm, CTRL+drag a rectangle over your base, confirm, and every qualifying building inside is queued at once.

Mirrors the game's own deconstruct rules — skips the HQ, non-deconstructable buildings, and anything already being torn down.

mods.name: Squad Move and Fire (experimental)
mods.summary: Squads fire while moving instead of halting — on foot and in vehicles. Hold Your Ground truly anchors.

sections.description:
EXPERIMENTAL (v0.1.2). Squads fire WHILE moving instead of stopping to shoot — on foot and in vehicles (needs Fire-at-Will + Move-at-Will stances). Hold Your Ground now truly anchors a squad: it fires in-range targets but won't advance to engage.

Adds an accuracy = damage model (IFZ has no hit-roll): penalties for walking/running/driving, a hard penalty while swimming, a confidence bonus firing from inside buildings, and a penalty when swarmed in the open. All tunable in F1.

mods.summary: At night, mortars autonomously lob illumination flares at dark infected clusters, revealing fog-of-war.
mods.fileNotes: Fixed in 0.1.7: flares clear fog again after loading a save mid-session - they were dying at the top of their arc and revealing nothing. No config changes.

sections.description:
EXPERIMENTAL (v0.1.3). At night, bunker- and squad-mounted mortars autonomously lob an illumination flare at dark infected clusters within range. It arcs up, airbursts, and parachutes down as a red light + thin smoke trail, revealing the fog-of-war beneath it so mortars can engage the lit horde.

Built as a pure driver + reflection (no Harmony). Flare stack is per-save.
